conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me first review the official eligibility criteria, and then I'll ask you some questions about your circumstances.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for waiting. I've reviewed the eligibility criteria. To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit, I need to ask you some questions about your circumstances.

First, do you have any children you're responsible for?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do! I have a child - my son Alex. He's 15 years old and lives with me at home.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. It's helpful to know about Alex.

Do you live in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - I'm in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's great, thank you. 

Is Alex currently in approved education or training (such as attending school)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, he's still at school - he's only 15, so he's in his GCSEs at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent, thank you for confirming that.

Just a few more questions to complete the assessment: Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him. It's just me looking after him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. One final question: Do you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000 per year? 

(This won't affect your eligibility to receive Child Benefit, but it may mean you'd need to pay a High Income Child Benefit tax charge.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I'd rather not get into the details of my income right now - I'm mainly just trying to find out if I'm eligible to claim it in the first place.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's absolutely fine - I have enough information to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit.

Based on what you've told me, let me provide you with my assessment:

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

You meet all the eligibility criteria:
- You live in the UK
- Alex is under 16 (he's 15)
- He lives with you
- You're responsible for him
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for him

You can proceed with making a claim for Child Benefit. Just as a note for your information: even if you or a partner earn over £60,000 per year, you would still be eligible to claim Child Benefit, but you may need to pay a High Income Child Benefit tax charge. That's something you can look into separately if it's relevant to your situation.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 15 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 15 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 15 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex is 15 years old (under 16), lives with you in the UK, is in approved education (attending school for his GCSEs), and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for him. According to the eligibility rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 and you live in the UK. You'll usually be responsible for a child if you live with them, which you do. All the eligibility criteria are met for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK (Manchester) with their 15-year-old son Alex, who is in approved education (attending school). No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. The claimant meets all the basic eligibility requirements for Child Benefit.

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 15 years old (under 16), lives with you in the UK, is in approved education (attending school for his GCSEs), and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for him. According to the eligibility rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 and you live in the UK. You'll usually be responsible for a child if you live with them, which you do. All the eligibility criteria are met for Alex.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK (Manchester) with their 15-year-old son Alex, who is in approved education (attending school). No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. The claimant meets all the basic eligibility requirements for Child Benefit.
